LAW AND MOTION 301, PETITIONER CENTER FOR BIOLOGICAL DIVERSITY'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES - PETITIONER SATISFIES THE REQUIREMENTS OF CCP 1021.5 AND IS ENTITLED TO FEES FOR ALL PHASES OF ITS WORK. THE COURT CONCLUDES, HOWEVER, THAT PLAINTIFF'S SPENT MORE THAN A REASONABLE AMOUNT OF TIME ON CERTAIN TASKS AND THEREFORE DEDUCTS 25 HOURS OF MR. LOARIE'S TIME FROM WORK ON THE OPENING MERITS BRIEF, 15 HOURS FROM THE MERITS REPLY, AND 25 HOURS FROM THE OPENING FEE BRIEF, FOR A TOTAL DEDUCTION OF $22,750, LEAVING A FEE AND COST AWARD IN THE AMOUNT OF $260,186.43, WHICH THE COURT CONSIDERS A REASONABLE AMOUNT FOR THE WORK PERFORMED SUBJECT TO THE CHECKING OF THE MATHEMATICAL FIGURE. 65 HOURS OUT OF MR. LOARIE'S TIME AT $375/HOUR WILL BE DEDUCTED FROM THE TOTAL IN THE APPENDIX IN THE REPLY BRIEF. MR. LOARIE IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR # 5797

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. GREGORY C. LOARIE OF EARTHJUSTICE, TEL. NO. (510) 550-6725 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER AND MOVING PARTY;
ATTY. DANIEL S. HARRIS, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 703-5530 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONER CENTER FOR BIOLOGICAL DIVERSITY'S MOTION For Attorney'S Fees - PETITIONER SATISFIES THE REQUIREMENTS OF C C P 1021.5 AND IS ENTITLED TO FEES FOR ALL PHASES OF ITS WORK. THE COURT CONCLUDES, HOWEVER, THAT PLAINTIFF'S SPENT MORE THAN A REASONABLE AMOUNT OF TIME ON CERTAIN TASKS AND THEREFORE DEDUCTS 25 HOURS OF MR. LOARIE'S TIME FROM WORK ON THE OPENING MERITS BRIEF, 15 HOURS FROM THE MERITS REPLY, AND 25 HOURS FROM THE OPENING FEE BRIEF, FOR A TOTAL DEDUCTION OF $22,750, LEAVING A FEE AND COST AWARD IN THE AMOUNT OF $260,186.43, WHICH THE COURT CONSIDERS A REASONABLE AMOUNT FOR THE WORK PERFORMED SUBJECT TO THE CHECKING OF THE MATHEMATICAL FIGURE. 65 HOURS OUT OF MR. LOARIE'S TIME AT $375/HOUR WILL BE DEDUCTED FROM THE TOTAL IN THE APPENDIX IN THE REPLY BRIEF. Mr. Loarie is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR # 5797 =301/PJB
